title: NACK
description: Payload for message_type=NACK. Any agent may send a NACK when it receives an invalid envelope or payload. A NACK blocks the run stage until a corrected message arrives. The sender of the invalid message MUST correct and re-send — it must NOT proceed.

missing_fields.description: JSON paths to fields that are missing or invalid (e.g. ['payload.interactions', 'payload.open_questions']). May be empty for non-field errors like 'unknown_message_type'.

expected_schema.description: The schema ID that the rejected message should conform to (e.g. 'management-team/goal-spec v1.0').

retry_instructions.description: Plain-language instructions for the sender to fix the message. Must be actionable. 'Provide the missing fields exactly; do not proceed.' is the minimum.

rejected_message_id.description: The message_id of the message being rejected. Required so the sender can identify which message to fix.

rejected_message_type.description: The message_type of the rejected message. Required to allow the sender to locate the correct message.
